title: 7 Creating and Managing Currency Orders in Cash Services
body_text:
7	Creating and Managing Currency Orders in Cash Services
7.1	Overview of Creating and Managing Currency Orders in Cash Services
This chapter is for Admin Users and Regular Users who create and manage regular cash orders in Cash Services. A maximum of one currency order per location, per delivery schedule, is permitted.
The Cash Services application allows users to create regular orders and delete single occurrences of standing orders. See Chapter 9, Standing Orders, Late Orders, and Contingency Orders, for procedures on creating orders outside of Cash Services.
Currency orders are extracted on business days at 11:00 am (local time). Your financial institutionâs armoured car company picks up the order from the Royal Bank cash centre on the dispatch date and delivers it to your financial institution location on the delivery date.
7.2	Creating Currency Orders in Cash Services
You can order regular and new Canadian notes in Cash Services as well as Canadian coin and US notes. New bank notes are available in Canadian currency only and are provided in bundles of 100 notes per denomination.
Note:	New bank note orders are subject to additional fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725, for fees. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
To enter an order in Cash Services:
Log in to the application as described in Section 5.2, Accessing Cash Services via the Client Centre.
Select
Management
>
Order Management
>
Order Entry
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Creating an Order Entry
The Order Entry page consists of several sections as shown in Figure 2 to Figure 5, including an Order Summary, shown in Figure 6. To create a currency order, begin with the steps in Figure 2.
â
Customer
â This field is
automatically
populated with the name of your financial institution.
â¡
Location
â If you only have access to one location, the Location field is pre-populated. If you have access to more than one location, select a delivery location from the drop-down menu.
â¢
Amount
â Enter the Amount for the cash order.
â£
Carrier Location and Run
â these fields are displayed for information purposes, based on the location profile.
â¤
Delivery
â Select a delivery date from the calendar. The delivery date is the date the currency arrives at the location.
By default, the system presents the earliest available delivery date according to the standard turnaround time for that location. If this date isnât the scheduled day of the week your armoured carrier delivers to this location, you can select a different delivery date from the calendar. See Section 10.3, Currency Order Processing Dates, for more information. If you miss the 11:00 am cut-off time for delivery, you can submit a late order request as described in Section 9.4, Creating Late Orders in ServiceNow.
â¥
Order Type
â The Order Type defaults to Regular. Currently, only regular orders are created in Cash Services. See Chapter 9, Standing Orders, Late Orders, and Contingency Orders, for procedures on requesting other types of orders.
â¦
Dispatch
â The dispatch date
automatically
populates when you select a delivery date. The dispatch date is the date the currency leaves the Royal Bank cash centre.
â§
Capture Comments
(optional)
â This field can be used to enter any relevant information for the employees receiving the parcel at your member location (e.g., âfor ATM 123â).
Figure 2: Entering Location, Amount, and Delivery Date
In the Categories section on the Order Currency page, you can choose any combination of Canadian notes (regular or new), Canadian coin, and US Notes. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Currency Categories
Select the drop-down arrow for CAD, COIN, CAD NEW, or US notes and enter the number of units per denomination. See Figure 4 and Figure 5.
Figure 4: Example of Units for Canadian Notes
Figure 5: Example of Units for Canadian Coin
Check the Order Summary to confirm that the total amount of the denominations and/or coin ordered matches the total order amount. If there is a discrepancy, change the denominations or revise the total in the Amount field. See Figure 6.
Select
Submit
, select
Yes
to confirm the submission, and select
OK
to acknowledge the confirmation number.
Figure 6: Submitting the Order
Related Information
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
7.3	Managing Currency Orders in Cash Services
Admin or Regular Users can view, edit, or delete regular orders in the Pending & Ready category in Cash Services. After the cut-off at 11:00 am local time, one business day before the dispatch date, orders are moved to the Carrier Check & Released category, where they can be viewed, but no longer edited or deleted.
Note:	A standing order canât be edited in Cash Services, but you can delete an occurrence if necessary.
To manage submitted cash orders:
Log in to the application as described in Section 5.2, Accessing Cash Services via the Client Centre.
Select
Management
>
Order Management
>
Manage Orders
. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Managing Orders
Viewing, Editing, and Deleting Pending & Ready Orders
The pending and ready orders are automatically displayed. Enter search criteria if you are looking for a specific order based on location or delivery date, then select
Filter
.
Select the âCONF IDâ number of an order to view the original order page.
To edit an order:
Select the pencil icon in the Actions column to open the order page for editing. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Select an Order for Editing
Make the necessary revisions, then select
Submit
.
Select
Yes
to confirm. The system will provide a confirmation number. When an order has been revised, you will see an alarm clock icon in the Actions column. Select this icon to view a pop-up of the Order History showing what changes were made to the order. In this example, the amount was changed. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: View Changes Made to an Order
To delete an order:
Select the check box beside the âCONF IDâ number of the appropriate order, then select
Delete
. See Figure 10.
Note:	If you delete a Standing Order, only that occurrence is deleted. To permanently delete a Standing Order, you must follow the workflow in ServiceNow. See Section 9.3, Creating and Deleting Standing Orders in ServiceNow.
Figure 10: Delete an Order in the Pending & Ready Category
Provide a reason for deleting the order, then select
Proceed
. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Provide Reason for Deleting an Order
Viewing Carrier Check & Released Orders
Select
Carrier Check & Released
.
Enter search criteria if you are looking for an order based on location and/or specific dates. See Figure 12.
Note:	If you are entering a date range, the delivery date is considered the âfromâ date.
Check the box beside âCheck Historical Orders?â, then select
Filter
to display the orders.
Select the âCONF IDâ number of an order to see the details of the processed order.
Note:	Orders in Carrier Check & Released canât be edited or deleted.
Figure 12: Orders in the Carrier Check & Released Category
END
